original_text: New York passes legislation requiring paid sick leave for all private-sector employees.
identified_property: generosity and comprehensiveness of the sick leave benefit

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring up to 3 days of unpaid sick leave annually for private-sector employees only at companies with 100 or more workers.

variations.label: low
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring up to 5 days of partially paid sick leave at half wages annually for private-sector employees at companies with 15 or more workers.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring paid sick leave for all private-sector employees, but limited to 5 days annually with a 90-day waiting period before eligibility.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring paid sick leave for all private-sector employees.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring paid sick leave for all private-sector employees, with full wage replacement and the ability to use leave to care for a sick family member.

variations.label: high
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring generous paid sick leave for all private-sector employees, with full wage replacement, coverage for family caregiving and mental health needs, and strong job-protection guarantees.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: New York passes legislation requiring comprehensive paid sick leave for all private-sector employees, with full wage replacement from day one, uncapped accrual, coverage for physical health, mental health, and family caregiving needs, and robust job-protection and anti-retaliation provisions.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier adds meaningful generosity: Tier 1 is unpaid and limited to large firms; Tier 2 adds partial pay and broader firm coverage; Tier 3 introduces full pay but with restrictions; Tier 4 is standard paid sick leave; Tier 5 adds full wage replacement and family care; Tier 6 adds mental health coverage and job protection; Tier 7 maximizes all dimensions including uncapped accrual and anti-retaliation protections. The core scenario—New York sick leave legislation—remains constant throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it provides the most generous and comprehensive sick leave benefit: full wage replacement from day one, no cap on accrual, broad coverage spanning physical health, mental health, and family caregiving, plus the strongest worker protections against retaliation—maximizing the well-being and security of employees.
